onSuccess.consequence:
A red LED lights up on the board. The screen switches from the newscast being broadcast and shows static for a moment then stabilises on an office scene.
"--no, I'm telling you the northern districts won't last the month." "Quiet. The diverted grain shipments are classified. Put the real manifests in Archive Room B. We'll have O'Brien check the numbers but the public numbers stay fixed for now."
A chair scrapes. Paper rustles.
"If anyone sees the originals - " "Shh! We have to keep them so we know exactly what the real situation is, you know that!"
One of the figures picks up a folder and leaves the room. The other turns back to his desk.
You realise you're watching senior Party members inside the Ministry of Plenty itself.

onFailed.consequence: The red LED lights up on the board. The screen switches from the newscast being broadcast and shows static for a moment then stabilises on a Minitrue office. You watch for several minutes, but everyone keeps their heads down. Nothing sensitive is said.

onSuccess.consequence:
A red LED lights up on the board. The screen switches from the newscast being broadcast and shows static for a moment then stabilises to a military operations room somewhere in Minipax. This must be somewhere in Minipax.
A black overalled Inner Party member is talking with a blue overalled subordinate.
"We redeployed the Fifth Division west, not south."
"The bulletin says south. It was approved hours ago."
"The men could be in danger!"
"Do you need a reminder of what happens in Miniluv? It's true if the Party says it's true. Update the board and file the orders in the Map Room vault. The Party may need to know where the men really are. If it suits us."
The blue overalled subordinate nods, clearly cowed by the threat of Miniluv. "Of course, Sir. Right away."

onFailed.consequence: The red LED lights up on the board. The screen switches from the newscast being broadcast and shows static for a moment then stabilises to the military operations room somewhere in Minipax again. You watch for some time but the figure in black overalls has left, analysts move between map tables, but speak only in clipped whispers you can’t make out.

onSuccess.consequence:
A red LED lights up on the board as it hums into life. The screen goes blank, briefly showing a menu then flickers to show a Ministry of Truth editorial desk.
"We can't keep rewriting the peace treaty every week."
"If the current one we put out is wrong, it's wrong. Burn the old edition. Furnace Wing. The new text goes to Distribution as soon as possible."
"We are at war with Eurasia. We've always been at war with Eurasia."
"Where are we with the prisoner transfers?"
"The next consignment is due to arrive on Thursday." "Make sure there's photos and file them in Records Room 26. We need photos of all thought criminals."

onFailed.consequence: The red LED lights up on the board. The screen switches from the newscast being broadcast and shows static for a moment then stabilises to the Ministry of Truth editorial room again. Figures in blue overalls are working at their desks. You watch for some time but nobody speaks.

onSuccess.consequence:
A red LED lights up on the board and the screen cuts to a depot office - typewriters, clanking pipes, irritated voices.
"These fuel quotas don't match field consumption - we use far more." "This is what we're told to release. Public reports mustn't show the real situation. The real ledgers go to Depot 9 for storage. The Party will plan accordingly. Don't question instructions."
The clerk's typewriter hammers pause for a moment, then resume with mechanical force.

onFailed.consequence: The red LED lights up on the board. The screen switches from the newscast being broadcast and shows static for a moment then stabilises on the depot office. The clerk is typing and blue overalled figures are busying to and fro. You watch for some time but there is little more to learn.
